goal: Open Phase 1 Week 5 by writing the first production plugin manifest (Serena, category=structural) and the `ucil plugin install <name>` CLI command that spawns Serena's MCP server, runs a real `tools/list` probe via the existing `PluginManager::health_check` infrastructure, and asserts the response contains ≥10 tools — thereby satisfying the P1-W5-F01 frozen acceptance `scripts/verify/P1-W5-F01.sh` end-to-end with no mocks of Serena.
plan_summary: Three coordinated deliverables, all independent of the in-flight WO-0006 / WO-0008 / WO-0012 branches. Part 1 (`plugins/structural/serena/plugin.toml`): a real manifest whose `[transport]` section launches Serena's upstream MCP server via `uvx --from git+https://github.com/oraios/serena@main serena-mcp-server --context ide-assistant --project .` (the canonical install per the Serena README — executor must re-check the upstream docs at fetch-time and pin the `@<ref>` to a known-good tag/commit instead of a moving `main` if the README recommends one). Part 2 (`crates/ucil-cli/src/commands/plugin.rs`, new module): a clap `PluginArgs` enum with an `install <name>` subcommand that (a) walks `plugins_dir` (default `./plugins`) for any directory containing a `plugin.toml` whose `[plugin] name == <name>`; (b) parses that manifest via `PluginManifest::from_path`; (c) calls a NEW public `PluginManager::health_check_with_timeout(manifest, timeout_ms)` function added in Part 3 with a caller-supplied budget (default 180_000 ms to cover uvx cold-start fetches); (d) prints the tool count + tool names as JSON to stdout; (e) exits 0 iff `status == HealthStatus::Ok` AND `tools.len() >= 1` (the CLI contract) — the `≥ 10` Serena-specific floor is enforced by the verify script, not the CLI. Part 3 (`crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs`, additive only): introduce `pub async fn health_check_with_timeout(manifest: &PluginManifest, timeout_ms: u64) -> Result<PluginHealth, PluginError>` that mirrors the existing `health_check` body but accepts a caller-supplied `Duration::from_millis(timeout_ms)`; the existing `pub async fn health_check` delegates to it with `HEALTH_CHECK_TIMEOUT_MS`. No other public-API change; `test_hot_cold_lifecycle` still passes unmodified. Part 4 (`scripts/verify/P1-W5-F01.sh`): a real bash acceptance that (a) checks for `uvx` in PATH, errors with a clear "requires uvx (or Docker fallback — future WO)" message if absent; (b) pre-warms by running `uvx --from git+https://github.com/oraios/serena@<ref> serena-mcp-server --help` to cache the Python deps (swallow stdout/stderr); (c) `cargo build --release -p ucil-cli`; (d) invokes `./target/release/ucil plugin install serena --plugins-dir ./plugins --timeout-ms 180000 --format json`; (e) parses the JSON output with `jq`, asserts `tools | length >= 10` (the Serena floor). Unit test coverage in `plugin.rs` exercises the manifest-resolution path using the existing `mock-mcp-plugin` binary shipped by `ucil-daemon` (the unit test spawns a TempDir with a dummy plugin.toml pointing at `target/<profile>/mock-mcp-plugin`, runs `install` end-to-end, asserts `tools == ["list_repos"]`) — that is NOT a mock of Serena, it is a real MCP subprocess serving as the subject-under-test for the CLI plumbing.
scope_in: `plugins/structural/serena/plugin.toml` — NEW file (category directory `structural/` already exists). `[plugin]` section: `name = "serena"`, `version = "1.0.0"`, `description = "LSP-powered symbol navigation for 40+ languages (oraios/serena)."`. `[transport]` section: `type = "stdio"`, `command = "uvx"`, `args = ["--from", "git+https://github.com/oraios/serena@<REF>", "serena-mcp-server", "--context", "ide-assistant", "--project", "."]` — executor must replace `<REF>` with a concrete commit-SHA or released tag. A moving `main` is forbidden per .claude/rules/commit-style.md implicit reproducibility. `[lifecycle]` section: `hot_cold = true`, `idle_timeout_minutes = 10` (matches master-plan §14.1 default guidance).

`crates/ucil-cli/src/commands/plugin.rs` — NEW module. Public API: `pub struct PluginArgs` (a `clap::Args` holding a `clap::Subcommand` field of type `PluginSubcommand`), `pub enum PluginSubcommand { Install(InstallArgs) }`, `pub struct InstallArgs { name: String, plugins_dir: PathBuf (default "./plugins"), timeout_ms: u64 (default 180_000), format: OutputFormat (default Text) }`, and `pub async fn run(args: PluginArgs) -> anyhow::Result<()>`. Support: recursive walk of `plugins_dir` for *all* `plugin.toml` files (max depth 3), parse each via `PluginManifest::from_path`, match by `[plugin] name`, error with `PluginError::NotFound(name)` if zero matches OR `PluginError::AmbiguousName(name, [paths])` if ≥2. Errors use `thiserror::Error` on a new `PluginCmdError` enum (local to the module); `run` returns `anyhow::Result<()>` via `.context(...)` since main.rs is the `anyhow` boundary per .claude/rules/rust-style.md.

`crates/ucil-cli/src/commands/plugin.rs` — on successful health check, emit one of: (i) text mode (default): `println!("plugin `serena` ACTIVE: 47 tools")` followed by `for name in &tools { println!("  - {name}"); }`; (ii) JSON mode (`--format json`): serde-serialize `{"name": str, "status": "ok|degraded|error", "tools": [str], "tool_count": int}` to stdout via `serde_json::to_writer_pretty(std::io::stdout(), ...)`. JSON mode is consumed by `scripts/verify/P1-W5-F01.sh`.

`crates/ucil-cli/src/main.rs` — add `Plugin(commands::plugin::PluginArgs)` variant to `enum Commands` (right after `Init`); add the match arm `Commands::Plugin(args) => commands::plugin::run(args).await`. Preserve the existing `Init` wiring byte-for-byte.

`crates/ucil-cli/Cargo.toml` — add `ucil-daemon = { path = "../ucil-daemon" }` under `[dependencies]`; add `serde_json.workspace = true` already present; verify `walkdir = "2"` or `ignore = "0.4"` as a directory-walk dep (prefer `walkdir` for simplicity; add to workspace if absent). Keep dev-deps (`tempfile`) as-is.

`crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs` — **additive only**. Add `pub async fn health_check_with_timeout(manifest: &PluginManifest, timeout_ms: u64) -> Result<PluginHealth, PluginError>` whose body is the *exact body* of the existing `health_check` with `HEALTH_CHECK_TIMEOUT` replaced by `Duration::from_millis(timeout_ms)`. Rewire existing `health_check` to `Self::health_check_with_timeout(manifest, HEALTH_CHECK_TIMEOUT_MS).await` so the net behavior of the old API is byte-identical — confirmed by the fact that `test_hot_cold_lifecycle` MUST still pass unmodified after this WO lands. Re-export `health_check_with_timeout` via `pub use plugin_manager::...` in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/lib.rs`. Rustdoc the new fn with `# Errors` identical to the existing `health_check` plus one additional sentence: `The timeout is user-supplied; callers on slow-uvx-install paths should budget ≥30s on first-run.`

`crates/ucil-cli/src/commands/plugin.rs` — module-root acceptance test `test_plugin_install_resolves_manifest_by_name` (frozen selector `commands::plugin::test_plugin_install_resolves_manifest_by_name`, exact match, module-root — NOT inside `mod tests { }`). Test body: (1) locate the `mock-mcp-plugin` binary via the same `current_exe()` heuristic WO-0010 established in `plugin_manager.rs:716`; (2) write a TempDir with `plugins_fixture/search/fakeplugin/plugin.toml` pointing `command = "<mock-path>"`; (3) call `plugin::run(InstallArgs { name: "fakeplugin".into(), plugins_dir: tmp.path().join("plugins_fixture"), timeout_ms: 5000, format: OutputFormat::Json })`; (4) capture stdout via a `gag::BufferRedirect::stdout()` alternative — or more simply, refactor `run` to take a `W: Write` writer (bumping the pub API); (5) assert exit Ok and the printed JSON contains `"status": "ok"` and `"tool_count": 1`. The `mock-mcp-plugin` binary already exists and answers tools/list with a single `list_repos` tool per plugin_manager.rs:750-800.

`crates/ucil-cli/src/commands/plugin.rs` — ONE additional supporting test INSIDE `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { … }` (module-prefix path `commands::plugin::tests::…` is fine for non-frozen selectors). Name: `tests::errors_on_unknown_name`. Body: writes an empty `plugins_fixture/`, invokes `run` with `name="serena"`, asserts the anyhow error message contains `"plugin 'serena' not found"`.

`scripts/verify/P1-W5-F01.sh` — overwrite the current TODO stub with a real acceptance script. Requirements: `#!/usr/bin/env bash` shebang, `set -euo pipefail`, `IFS=$'\n\t'`. Behaviour: (1) resolve repo-root via `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` and `cd` there; (2) assert `uvx` is in `$PATH` via `command -v uvx >/dev/null 2>&1` — on failure print `echo 'P1-W5-F01: requires uvx (install from https://github.com/astral-sh/uv). Serena docker fallback is a future WO.' >&2 && exit 1`; (3) pre-warm Serena: `uvx --from git+https://github.com/oraios/serena@<REF> serena-mcp-server --help >/dev/null 2>&1 || true` with a 120s timeout via `timeout 120 …`; (4) build: `cargo build --release -p ucil-cli --locked`; (5) invoke: `OUT=$(./target/release/ucil plugin install serena --plugins-dir ./plugins --timeout-ms 180000 --format json)`; (6) parse: `TOOL_COUNT=$(echo "$OUT" | jq -r '.tool_count')`, `STATUS=$(echo "$OUT" | jq -r '.status')`; (7) assert `STATUS == ok` AND `TOOL_COUNT -ge 10` using `[[ "$STATUS" == "ok" && "$TOOL_COUNT" -ge 10 ]] || { echo "FAIL: status=$STATUS tools=$TOOL_COUNT"; exit 1; }`; (8) `echo "P1-W5-F01 PASS: serena tools=$TOOL_COUNT"`. The script MUST exit 0 on success and non-zero with a clear reason on any failure. Header comment documents `uvx` prerequisite + the follow-up Docker-fallback work-order.

Per-crate rustdoc on every new `pub` item: `PluginArgs`, `PluginSubcommand`, `InstallArgs`, `OutputFormat`, `PluginManager::health_check_with_timeout`, `commands::plugin::run`. Each `Result`-returning pub fn includes a `# Errors` section per .claude/rules/rust-style.md. No `unwrap()` / `expect()` outside `#[cfg(test)]`.
Commits follow .claude/rules/commit-style.md with `Phase: 1`, `Feature: P1-W5-F01`, `Work-order: WO-0013` trailers.
scope_out: P1-W5-F02 (LSP JSON-RPC client) — DEFERRED; depends on this WO and on P1-W4-F05. Do NOT add any `textDocument/diagnostic` / `callHierarchy` / `typeHierarchy` wiring here.
P1-W5-F03-F09 — all downstream; do NOT pre-wire.
Wiring Serena into the daemon's startup sequence (that is part of P1-W3-F08 progressive startup — a separate WO reserved for after WO-0008 merges per WO-0011 planner notes).
Editing `crates/ucil-daemon/src/main.rs` — reserved for the progressive-startup integration WO.

Editing `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs`, `session_manager.rs`, `storage.rs`, `lifecycle.rs` — `storage.rs` and `lifecycle.rs` live on in-flight branches WO-0006 / WO-0008; touching them would create merge conflicts. `server.rs` and `session_manager.rs` are stable but unrelated to plugin-install semantics.
Editing `crates/ucil-treesitter/**`, `crates/ucil-core/src/knowledge_graph.rs` — unrelated; on other in-flight branches or stable.
Editing `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/**` — the crate exists but its lib.rs is an empty skeleton awaiting P1-W5-F02+. Do NOT add modules here in this WO.

Docker-backed Serena fixture — if `uvx` is absent on the verifier's machine, the acceptance script exits 1 with a clear message and P1-W5-F01 remains `passes=false`. A follow-up WO can add a Dockerfile / docker-compose harness for CI.

Mocking Serena — forbidden per root-CLAUDE.md invariant #1 ("no mocks of critical deps"). The `mock-mcp-plugin` binary is used only in the unit test, and ONLY as the subject-under-test for the CLI-plumbing path; the Serena feature acceptance uses real upstream Serena via uvx.

Adding a `--docker` flag or any Docker-runtime invocation on the CLI `install` subcommand — the transport is `stdio` per plugin.toml `[transport]`; whether the command is `uvx` or `docker` is a manifest concern, not a CLI concern.

Modifying `PluginManager::health_check` default timeout — the existing 5_000 ms is preserved for HOT/COLD acceptance test semantics; the new `health_check_with_timeout` is the only way to pass a larger budget.
acceptance: P1-W5-F01: `bash scripts/verify/P1-W5-F01.sh` exits 0 when `uvx` is available — asserts Serena tools/list returned ≥10 tools via the real Serena MCP server.
`ucil plugin install serena --plugins-dir ./plugins --format json` on a machine with `uvx` installed: prints a JSON object with `status: "ok"` and `tool_count >= 10`; exits 0.
`ucil plugin install <unknown>` exits non-zero with an error message containing `not found`.
`ucil plugin install <ambiguous>` (two plugin.toml files share the same `[plugin] name`) exits non-zero with `ambiguous`.
`cargo nextest run -p ucil-cli commands::plugin::test_plugin_install_resolves_manifest_by_name` — exactly one passing test (real `mock-mcp-plugin` subprocess).
`cargo nextest run -p ucil-cli` — all new + prior (`init::*`) tests pass.
`cargo nextest run -p ucil-daemon plugin_manager::test_hot_cold_lifecycle` — still passes (additive `health_check_with_timeout` must not regress).
`cargo nextest run -p ucil-daemon plugin_manager::` — all prior plugin_manager tests pass.
No mocks of Serena MCP in the production code, in the unit test, or in the verify script — the mock-mcp-plugin is the SUBJECT of the unit test's CLI plumbing, not a stand-in for Serena.
acceptance_criteria: bash scripts/verify/P1-W5-F01.sh exits 0 on a uvx-equipped machine (exit 1 with a clear message when uvx is missing; acceptable for dev envs, makes the P1-W5-F01 feature passes=false until the verifier runs on a uvx/Docker-equipped machine).
cargo nextest run -p ucil-cli commands::plugin::test_plugin_install_resolves_manifest_by_name --no-fail-fast — exits 0, exactly 1 test resolved and passed.
cargo nextest run -p ucil-cli --no-fail-fast — all ucil-cli tests pass (new + existing `init::*`).
cargo nextest run -p ucil-daemon plugin_manager::test_hot_cold_lifecycle --no-fail-fast — still exits 0 (regression guard for the additive change to plugin_manager.rs).
cargo nextest run -p ucil-daemon --no-fail-fast — all prior ucil-daemon tests still pass.
cargo clippy -p ucil-cli --all-targets -- -D warnings exits 0 (pedantic + nursery clean).

grep -RInE 'todo!\(|unimplemented!\(|NotImplementedError|raise NotImplementedError' crates/ucil-cli/src/commands/plugin.rs plugins/structural/serena/plugin.toml scripts/verify/P1-W5-F01.sh returns no matches.
grep -nE 'fn test_plugin_install_resolves_manifest_by_name' crates/ucil-cli/src/commands/plugin.rs — found at module level; line number must NOT lie inside any `mod tests {` block.
grep -nE 'fn health_check_with_timeout' crates/ucil-daemon/src/plugin_manager.rs returns at least one match (additive pub API).

grep -nE 'command\s*=\s*"uvx"' plugins/structural/serena/plugin.toml returns 1 match; the `args` array contains `serena-mcp-server`; `<REF>` has been replaced with a concrete git-ref (no literal `@main`, no literal `@<REF>`).
grep -nE 'HealthStatus::Ok|HealthStatus::Degraded|HealthStatus::Error' crates/ucil-cli/src/commands/plugin.rs — matches on all three branches of the status match in `run`.

scripts/reality-check.sh P1-W5-F01 — stashing plugins/structural/serena/plugin.toml + plugin.rs must make the verify script fail; popping must make it pass again. (Per-file rollback may false-positive on multi-file features per escalation 20260415-1630; manual verification by the verifier is the documented workaround.)
implementation_notes: SERENA UPSTREAM PINNING: the Serena repo is https://github.com/oraios/serena. Before committing `plugins/structural/serena/plugin.toml`, the executor MUST (a) curl the repo's README / releases via WebFetch, (b) pick the latest *tagged* release (format `v0.x.y`) OR, if no stable release exists, pin to the current `main` commit-SHA; (c) record the chosen ref in a top-of-file TOML comment (`# pinned 2026-04-17: tag vX.Y.Z`). A moving `main` breaks reproducibility and will be rejected.

UVX INVOCATION SHAPE: the canonical upstream-Serena install is `uvx --from git+https://github.com/oraios/serena@<ref> serena-mcp-server [flags]`. Flags the executor should preserve from the upstream README: `--context ide-assistant` (selects IDE-style tool surface), `--project .` (targets the working directory). The executor should NOT invent flags; cross-check against Serena's README before committing.

MODULE-ROOT TEST (WO-0006 / WO-0007 / WO-0010 / WO-0011 lesson): `test_plugin_install_resolves_manifest_by_name` uses the EXACT-match nextest selector `commands::plugin::test_plugin_install_resolves_manifest_by_name`. The test MUST live at module root in `crates/ucil-cli/src/commands/plugin.rs`, NOT inside any `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { }` wrapper. A supporting test (`tests::errors_on_unknown_name`) MAY live inside `mod tests { }` because its selector is non-frozen.

MOCK-MCP-PLUGIN IN CROSS-CRATE TEST: The unit test in `ucil-cli` needs the path to `target/<profile>/mock-mcp-plugin` (compiled by `ucil-daemon`). The test binary at `target/<profile>/deps/ucil_cli-<hash>` can `current_exe()?.parent()?.parent()?.join("mock-mcp-plugin")` to locate it. This only works when the mock is already built — the test MUST panic with a useful message if absent (e.g. `assert!(mock.exists(), "expected mock-mcp-plugin at {} — run `cargo build -p ucil-daemon --bin mock-mcp-plugin` first", mock.display())`). In `scripts/verify/P1-W5-F01.sh` this is irrelevant because the verify script uses real Serena, not the mock.

WRITER PARAMETERIZATION FOR TESTS: because the CLI needs to emit to stdout in production but be assertable in tests, refactor `run(args: PluginArgs)` into `run(args: PluginArgs) -> anyhow::Result<()>` (calls stdout) that internally delegates to `run_with_writer(args, writer: impl std::io::Write)`. The test calls `run_with_writer` with a `Vec<u8>`, then asserts against the captured bytes.

ADDITIVE PLUGIN_MANAGER EDIT: the new `health_check_with_timeout` MUST be purely additive. Sequence: (a) add the new fn with the full body; (b) rewire `health_check` to `Self::health_check_with_timeout(manifest, HEALTH_CHECK_TIMEOUT_MS).await`. Verify via `cargo nextest run -p ucil-daemon plugin_manager::test_hot_cold_lifecycle` that nothing regresses (the timeout is unchanged for existing call-sites).

CLIPPY PEDANTIC + NURSERY are already enabled on both crates. Anticipate lints: `clippy::doc_markdown` (wrap items in backticks), `clippy::missing_errors_doc` (populate `# Errors`), `clippy::must_use_candidate` (on `#[must_use]` on non-`()`-returning fns), `clippy::module_name_repetitions` (rename if needed or `#[allow]` with reason). Fix, do NOT blanket-allow without an ADR.

COMMIT CADENCE (target ~7 commits, each ≤200 lines of diff per DEC-0005): (1) `build(cli): add ucil-daemon + walkdir deps for ucil-cli`, (2) `feat(daemon): add PluginManager::health_check_with_timeout additive pub API`, (3) `feat(cli): add `plugin install` command with manifest resolution + health probe`, (4) `test(cli): add module-level test_plugin_install_resolves_manifest_by_name via mock-mcp-plugin`, (5) `test(cli): add unknown-name + ambiguous-name error tests`, (6) `feat(plugins): add structural/serena/plugin.toml pinned to Serena vX.Y.Z`, (7) `test(verify): implement scripts/verify/P1-W5-F01.sh real acceptance with uvx + jq`. DEC-0005 permits a single oversized commit for a coherent module file; if #3 exceeds 200 lines split #3 into (3a) the CLI types and (3b) the `run` logic.
NO `unwrap()` / `expect()` outside `#[cfg(test)]` (.claude/rules/rust-style.md). Use `?` and the anyhow context chain at the CLI boundary; the internal `PluginCmdError` is thiserror-derived.

TIMEOUT BUDGET: The 180_000 ms default is conservative; on a warm uvx cache Serena comes up in ~3-5s. On a cold uvx cache (first run after install) it can take 60-120s to download from GitHub + install Python deps. The verify-script pre-warm step amortises this.

VERIFY SCRIPT EXIT SEMANTICS: acceptance script MUST exit 0 ONLY when `tool_count ≥ 10`. Any earlier failure (missing uvx, build failure, CLI exit non-zero, parse error, `STATUS != ok`, `tool_count < 10`) exits non-zero with a one-line failure reason — the verifier relies on the exit code and stdout for its report.

JQ DEPENDENCY IN VERIFY SCRIPT: `jq` is used to parse the CLI's `--format json` output. `jq` is a standard dev tool but the script should assert `command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1` at the top and print an install hint on failure. Unlike `uvx`, `jq` is trivial to install (`apt-get install jq` / `brew install jq`) and shipping a jq-less parsing path is not worth the complexity.

CATEGORY CHOICE FOR SERENA: `plugins/structural/serena/` matches master-plan §14.1 example (`category = "quality"` for semgrep, `category = "structural"` for Serena) and the existing `plugins/structural/` directory skeleton. The PluginManifest struct does NOT currently parse a `category` field, so the directory layout is the sole encoding — do NOT add a `[plugin] category = "…"` field to the manifest (PluginManifest::from_path would ignore it silently via serde's default behavior anyway, but the cleaner signal is via directory placement).

lessons_applied: WO-0006/WO-0007/WO-0008/WO-0010/WO-0011 all taught: exact-match nextest selectors (`module::fn_name`) do NOT match tests nested inside `mod tests { }` blocks (which produce `module::tests::fn_name`). The frozen feature-list selector for P1-W5-F01 is a SCRIPT (`scripts/verify/P1-W5-F01.sh`), not a cargo-test selector — so the mod-tests-nesting trap does not apply to the feature's acceptance test. HOWEVER, the WO's own regression test `test_plugin_install_resolves_manifest_by_name` is NOT frozen by feature-list but the executor should still keep it at module root per the broader project pattern.

DEC-0001/DEC-0005 precedent: the new `commands/plugin.rs` is a single coherent module (types + impl + module-level test + optional `mod tests { }`). Keep #3 under the 200-line soft ceiling by extracting `run_with_writer` before the writer refactor; if it still exceeds, split as noted in commit cadence and cite DEC-0005 in the commit body.

DEC-0007: no cargo-mutants run is part of this WO's gate. Reality-check.sh stash-mutation is still in force; mutation must be confirmed by stashing the plugin.toml + plugin.rs + the new plugin_manager lines and asserting the verify script fails.
WO-0011 lesson: rusqlite pragma + WAL + BEGIN IMMEDIATE rules are NOT relevant to this WO (no SQLite involvement). The knowledge_graph.rs file is forbidden from edits here.

WO-0012 session-abandonment lesson (per `ucil-build/verification-reports/root-cause-WO-0012.md`): the executor MUST produce a non-empty commit sequence in the first 30-40 minutes of the session. The ONE clearest risk factor for this WO is the executor stalling on 'which Serena git-ref to pin to?' — if the WebFetch / README lookup feels ambiguous for more than 10 minutes, pin to `main` with a TODO comment and file an escalation rather than silently abandoning the session. NEVER end the session with an uncommitted working tree.

WO-0011 planner-notes: 'Avoid main.rs wiring: every Phase-1 WO so far has explicitly avoided editing `crates/ucil-daemon/src/main.rs`. The integration WO that wires Lifecycle + PluginManager + McpServer + KnowledgeGraph + watcher + indexing into a single startup sequence is reserved for P1-W3-F08 (progressive startup) — a separate WO once the prerequisite branches are merged.' — this WO honors that directive (main.rs remains untouched).

Prior planner rule (from WO-0011): 'while WO-0008 features … and WO-0006 features … are still passes=false, both are awaiting verifier re-run on existing branches (escalations resolved).' This WO forbids editing ucil-treesitter/** and the WO-0008-owned daemon files to avoid merge conflict when those branches merge.

New additive pub API in plugin_manager.rs: design lesson from master-plan §14 is that `PluginManager::health_check` is a stable contract consumed by both P1-W3-F06 acceptance (`test_hot_cold_lifecycle`) and this WO's CLI. Extending it additively (new `health_check_with_timeout`) preserves the 5_000 ms default and therefore protects the prior acceptance test. Attempting to parameterise the existing fn would be a breaking change requiring an ADR.
planner_notes: P1-W5-F01 is the only Phase-1 feature whose dependencies are met AND whose implementation is NOT already in flight on a WO-0006/WO-0008/WO-0012 branch. P1-W3-F08 (progressive startup, deps=P1-W3-F07 ✓) was deliberately deferred per the WO-0011 planner-notes until WO-0008 (Lifecycle) merges. P1-W4-F02/F08 are covered by the in-retry WO-0012. P1-W2-F02/F03/F06 (WO-0006) and P1-W3-F01/P1-W4-F07 (WO-0008) are awaiting verifier re-run on existing branches; no new WO is needed. Everything downstream of those remains blocked. This WO is a Week-5 kickoff that does NOT span >1 week of master-plan scope (every scope_in item is Week 5 or Week-3-plumbing extension). The planner explicitly avoided bundling P1-W5-F02+ (LSP JSON-RPC client, etc.) because those depend on P1-W4-F05 (find_definition), itself blocked on P1-W4-F04 ← P1-W4-F02 (in flight). No ADR required: master-plan §14.1 fully specifies plugin.toml; §13 fully specifies Serena's role; `PluginManager::health_check` already sends tools/list via real subprocess stdio. The single judgment call — the Serena upstream git-ref — is handled by the executor's fetch-time README lookup and documented pin in the manifest TOML. If Serena's upstream shape differs materially from the README (e.g. no `serena-mcp-server` entry-point), the executor raises an ADR rather than silently improvising.
